REIS IA SEMANTIC TOKENS DESIGN SYSTEM v1.0

Semantic Tokens

The complete semantic token mapping — from accent opacity ladders and signal colors through text, border, and shadow scales, to motion curves, durations, gradients, and z-index layers. Every token has a purpose-driven name that describes intent, not appearance.

[01] Accent Blue Opacity Ladder

12-step graduated opacity scale for composable blue accent usage across backgrounds, borders, shadows, and fills. Each step has a specific intended use documented below.

Two parallel ladders exist: --blue-XX (primary, used in implementation) and --accent-XX (alias, broader range). They share the same base color: rgba(74, 144, 255).

2%
Row/cell hover tint
4%
Ambient light pools
6%
Section ambient glow
8%
Subtle glow shadow
10%
Badge/tag background
12%
Blue glow shadow
15%
Card hover inset
20%
Badge border, accent border
30%
Border accent
40%
Strong accent fill, focus ring
50%
Rotating border highlight peak
80%
Near-solid accent
Token
Value
Usage
--blue-02
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.02)
Row/cell hover tint
--blue-04
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.04)
Ambient light pools
--blue-06
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.06)
Section ambient glow
--blue-08
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.08)
Subtle glow shadow
--blue-10
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.10)
Badge/tag background
--blue-12
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.12)
Blue glow shadow
--blue-15
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.15)
Card hover inset
--blue-20
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.20)
Badge border, accent border
--blue-30
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.30)
Border accent
--blue-40
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.40)
Strong accent fill, focus ring
--blue-50
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.50)
Rotating border highlight peak
--blue-80
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.80)
Near-solid accent
[02] Signal Colors

Signal colors for form validation, status indicators, and user feedback. These are the only non-blue, non-white colors in the system. Each signal has 3 tokens: foreground, background, and border.

Error
Foreground
#EF4444
Background
10%
Border
25%
Warning
Foreground
#F59E0B
Background
10%
Border
25%
Success
Foreground
#22C55E
Background
10%
Border
25%
Info
Foreground
#4A90FF
Background
10%
Border
25%
Error

This field is required.

Warning

Changes not saved yet.

Success

Changes saved.

Info

Tip: You can customize this.

[03] Text Opacity Scale

Text hierarchy is achieved through opacity of white, not through different gray hex values. This creates visual unity and ensures text always harmonizes with any surface tier.

Primary
#FFFFFF (100%)
Headlines and strong emphasis
Headlines, hero text, primary labels
Secondary
rgba(255,255,255, 0.70)
Body copy and descriptions
Body text, card descriptions, navigation
Tertiary
rgba(255,255,255, 0.50)
Captions, metadata, and labels
Captions, timestamps, secondary labels
Quaternary
rgba(255,255,255, 0.35)
Decorative text and disabled states
Placeholder text, disabled states, code comments
Muted
rgba(255,255,255, 0.20)
Watermark text and ghost elements
Background watermarks, ghost labels, barely visible
Key Rule

Never use raw hex grays (#666, #999, #ccc). Always use the opacity scale tokens. This ensures text naturally adapts to any surface background and maintains the unified visual system.

[04] Border Opacity Scale

Borders use white at varying opacities, ensuring they harmonize with any surface tier. One accent variant adds blue emphasis to featured elements.

5% white
Subtle
Ghost borders, barely visible
8% white
Default
Card borders, dividers
12% white
Visible
Hover borders
20% white
Strong
Active, focus borders
30% blue
Accent
Featured card borders
Token
Value
Usage
Subtle
rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05)
Ghost borders, barely visible structure
Default
rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.08)
Card borders, dividers, standard
Visible
rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12)
Hover borders, emphasized dividers
Strong
rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.20)
Active borders, input focus (non-blue)
Accent
rgba(74, 144, 255, 0.30)
Blue-tinted borders on featured cards
[05] Shadow System

Shadows are used sparingly. On dark backgrounds, elevation is communicated through surface layering, not shadows. Shadows are reserved for floating elements only. The blue glow adds brand emphasis.

Subtle
0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)
Minimal depth hint
Default
0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.25)
Dropdowns, floating panels
Elevated
0 8px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)
Modals, popovers
Dramatic
0 16px 70px rgba(0,0,0,0.5)
Full-screen overlays
Blue Glow
0 0 50px rgba(74,144,255,0.12)
Featured card hover, CTA emphasis
[06] Interactive State Tokens

Tokens for focus indicators, text selection, and other interactive states. These ensure consistent interactive behavior across all components.

Focus Ring
Focused element
--focus-ring: var(--blue-40)
Selection
Try selecting this highlighted text to see the selection color.
--selection-bg: var(--blue-30)
Hover Tint
List item 1 — hover me
List item 2 — hover me
List item 3 — hover me
--blue-02 for subtle row hover
[07] Gradient Tokens

Named gradient tokens for ambient effects, border accents, surface transitions, and utility patterns. Gradients are blue-tinted or surface-relative, never multi-color.

Blue Ambient
Radial glow behind key sections
Blue Sweep
Linear accent gradient for borders
Surface Fade
Vertical section transitions S-0 to S-1
Divider
Horizontal gradient dividers between sections
Marquee Edge
Horizontal carousel edge fade to S-0
[08] Easing Curves

5 custom easing curves, each engineered for specific interaction types. Click each demo to see the curve in action. No default CSS ease or linear — every transition uses a named curve.

[09] Duration Tokens

7 duration tiers, each roughly 1.5-2x the previous. Click each demo to see the timing in action. The bar fills over the exact duration of the token.

[10] Z-Index Layer System

8-layer z-index scale. Never use arbitrary z-index values — always reference these named layers. Higher values are closer to the viewer.

Toast
200 Toast notifications
Modal
100 Modal dialogs
Modal Overlay
90 Dark overlay behind modals
Dropdown
60 Nav dropdowns, tooltips
Navigation
50 Fixed navigation bar
Raised
10 Overlapping elements, sticky sections
Base
0 Default content
Background
-1 Decorative elements behind content
Key Rule

Never use z-index: 9999 or arbitrary values. The 8-layer system provides clear stacking order for every UI element. If you need a new layer, discuss with the design system maintainer.

[11] Container Width Tokens

Content width hierarchy. Each container has a semantic name matching its content type. The container padding token provides fluid horizontal padding.

Token
Value
Usage
Wide
1280px
Full-bleed sections, navigation
Standard
1200px
Primary content container
Headline
900px
Centered headlines max-width
Narrow
800px
Text-heavy sections, forms, FAQs
Text
680px
Body copy paragraphs (65-75 chars/line)
Padding
clamp(20px, 5vw, 48px)
Horizontal padding on all containers
[12] Icon Size Tokens

5-step icon size scale aligned with the spacing grid. All icons use these standard sizes for visual consistency.

xs
12px
sm
16px
md
20px
lg
24px
xl
32px
Token
Value
Usage
xs
12px
Inline micro-icons, status dots
sm
16px
Button icons, badges
md
20px
Default icon size, navigation
lg
24px
Card icons, section markers
xl
32px
Feature icons, hero elements
[13] Border Radius Tokens
Token
Value
Usage
None
0px
Sharp corners, full-bleed elements
sm
4px
Badges, tags, inline code
md
6px
Small buttons, inputs
base
8px
Default for most components
lg
10px
Large buttons, hero inputs
xl
12px
Cards, panels
2xl
16px
Glass cards, large panels
full
9999px
Pills, avatars, circles

Design System v1.0 — the single source of truth for the Reis IA brand identity, tokens, components, and patterns.
